values.placeholder: Enter the exact URL you want to analyze

assignments.name: rows
assignments.value: Keyword,Position,Volume,KD%,URL,Title Tag,Meta Description,Heading Tag,# of Words On Page,Keyword Present On Page?,Number of Times On Page

assignments.name: Number of Times KW Is Included

parameters.jsCode:
const input = items[0].json;

return [
  { json: { Title: "Keyword", "Competitor analysis": input["Keyword"] } },
  { json: { Title: "Competitor URL", "Competitor analysis": input["Competitor URL"] } },
  { json: { Title: "Title Tag", "Competitor analysis": input["Title Tag"] } },
  { json: { Title: "Meta Description", "Competitor analysis": input["Meta Description"] } },
  { json: { Title: "Heading Tag", "Competitor analysis": input["Heading Tag"] } },
  { json: { Title: "# of Words On Page", "Competitor analysis": input["# of Words On Page"] } },
  { json: { Title: "Keyword Included On Page", "Competitor analysis": input["Keyword Included On Page"] === "true" ? "Y" : "N" } },
  { json: { Title: "Number of Times KW Is Included", "Competitor analysis": input["Number of Times KW Is Included"] } },
  { json: { Title: "", "Competitor analysis": "" } } // Optional: blank row separator
];

messageValues.message:
Prompt:
You are an SEO expert.

I will send you structured competitor data in the form of an array of objects. Each object contains two fields:

"Title" (such as "Keyword", "Competitor URL", "Title Tag", etc.)

"Competitor analysis" (the actual data value)

The data is repeating in blocks, where each block belongs to a single competitor for a specific keyword. The same keyword may appear multiple times with different competitors.

Your job is to:

Extract all unique keywords from the "Keyword" entries.

Group all competitor data by keyword.

For each unique keyword, analyze its full competitor block(s) (usually 3 competitors per keyword).

Use that analysis to generate 5 clear, practical SEO recommendations on how to improve ranking for our given page.

Return a separate JSON object per unique keyword using the exact format below.

Required Output Format (strictly):

[
  {
    "keyword": "keter shed",
    "our_url": "https://example.com/our-keter-shed-page",
    "recommendations": [
      "Recommendation 1",
      "Recommendation 2",
      "Recommendation 3",
      "Recommendation 4",
      "Recommendation 5"
    ]
  },
  {
    "keyword": "resin storage",
    "our_url": "https://example.com/our-resin-storage-page",
    "recommendations": [
      "Recommendation 1",
      "Recommendation 2",
      "Recommendation 3",
      "Recommendation 4",
      "Recommendation 5"
    ]
  }
]


Important Instructions:

If the same keyword appears more than once (e.g. "keter shed" repeated 5 times), only return one JSON block for that keyword.

Each keyword's competitor data must be fully analyzed before writing recommendations.

Your recommendations should focus on content quality, keyword usage, technical SEO, and user experience.

Do not skip any unique keyword. Return one complete JSON object per keyword.

Keep the field names and structure exactly as shown.

It is important to keep JSON structure consistent

parameters.subject: YTop 20 Keywords In Striking Distance Report Is Ready
parameters.message:
=

The Top 20 Keywords In Striking Distance workflow has finished running. All data has been processed and the results have been uploaded to the designated Google Sheet. Please review the updated spreadsheet here.

parameters.subject: SEMrush Report – No Keyword Data Found for Submitted URL
parameters.message:
=

Hello,

no keyword data was found for the URL ({{ $('Code').first().json.url }}) you submitted for analysis.

SEMrush returned the following response: "ERROR 50 :: NOTHING FOUND"
This typically means the submitted URL is not currently ranking for any keywords within the selected search database or the excluded filter applied.

If you would like to analyze a different URL, please feel free to resubmit the form.

parameters.subject: =No Keywords Matched for {{ $('Edit Fields').first().json.query_url }}
parameters.message:
=

Hello,

This is an automated notification to let you know that no keywords matched for the query URL:

{{ $('Edit Fields').first().json.query_url }}

SEMrush returned no results that meet the set conditions:
- Exact host+path match required
- Position between 5 and 15
- Search volume ≥ 500

Please review the query URL and conditions, and try again with a different input if needed.
